title: Circuit Reasoning: Stuck-at Fault Diagnosis
description: Diagnose a likely stuck-at fault from expected and observed logic behavior.
prompt_text: A simple circuit is supposed to compute Y = A AND B. During testing, the observed outputs are: A=0,B=0 gives Y=0; A=0,B=1 gives Y=1; A=1,B=0 gives Y=0; A=1,B=1 gives Y=1. Compare the expected behavior to the observed behavior and identify the most likely single stuck-at fault. Explain your reasoning.
general_guidance: Compare expected and observed outputs before naming the fault. A strong answer explains which test cases are normal and which one reveals the fault.
constraints: State the expected output for each input combination
Identify which observed row is inconsistent with an AND gate
Name the most likely stuck-at fault
Explain why other rows still appear correct

expert_answers.answer: For a correct AND gate, the output should be 0 for 00, 0 for 01, 0 for 10, and 1 for 11. The observed table only disagrees at A=0,B=1, where the circuit outputs 1 even though an AND gate should output 0. A likely single fault is that input A is stuck at 1 before the AND gate. If A is stuck high, the circuit effectively outputs B: when B=0 the output is 0, and when B=1 the output is 1. That matches all four observed rows. This is more precise than saying the whole output is stuck high, because the output is still 0 when B=0.
